untranslatable_reason: Signal requires calculation of mean and standard deviation of price changes over a dynamic lookback period to define volatility regimes. The allowed indicator set does not include a custom volatility calculation tool or standard deviation of returns indicator. While hvol_20 exists, it is a fixed 20-day historical volatility that does not support the flexible 10-30+ day lookback period or the specific mean ± 0.25 SD threshold logic required by this signal. The signal is a volatility regime classifier (NEUTRAL direction) rather than a directional trading rule, and cannot be expressed as entry/exit conditions using only the available indicators.
translation_notes: This signal defines volatility regime boundaries rather than traditional entry/exit points. To implement, a custom indicator calculating returns_std_dev over configurable periods would be required. The low volatility threshold (mean + 0.25 SD) could theoretically use bb_bandwidth if interpreted as volatility proxy, but this would be inaccurate. Signal is better suited as a regime filter overlay rather than standalone rule.
